AWS Full-Estate Coverage Audit

Every AWS resource type that can be created — the CloudFormation resource registry, the authoritative “what exists” spine — measured against what this tool collects and what it draws on the diagram. Measured against the whole registry, not a self-selected subset, so nothing hides.

Generated 2026-07-07 · CloudFormation spec v255.0.0 · 1622 resource types · 281 services. Regenerate with npm run coverage.

1622
Resource types
148
Detailed collectors
941
Inventory only
533
Not collected at all
How to read “Collected”: Detailed = dedicated collector with typed attributes. Inventory (untagged-catch) = one of the Cloud Control sweep types, caught regardless of tags. Inventory — if tagged = caught by the Resource Groups Tagging API only if the resource carries a tag; untagged instances are invisible. Not collected = no collector and not taggable — never seen.
“Displayed”: node = its own box on a graph view · edge = only a connecting line/route · panel-only = searchable list / details pane, never on the graph · not displayed.
